beer.beer_description: A darker beer fermented at a sligtly warmer temperature. "Pre-prohibition beers" were full of character and flavour as they were brewed using 'Floor-malted' barley - a process that disappeared in the 1920's as maltings became more mechanised. The use of floor-malted grains gives the beer its nutty or bready character, balanced with a generous gift of Czech grown Saaz hops in the kettle.

items.badge_description: It takes the perfect amount of light, the right framing and a touch of creative love to get the perfect picture of your brew. Now it’s time to drink it! That’s 185 check-ins with a photo.
Do you come from the land down under, where beer does flow, and men chunder? That's at least 160 different beers from Australia. Try 5 more for Level 33.

items.checkin_comment: Mystified as to how authentic this is to the billed style, because it's super obscure here in Oz, but you wouldn't kick it out of bed.

items.comment: That makes two of us! The recipe was from a "very reputable source". We are pretty happy with it!

items.badge_description: Throughout history, you’ll find plenty of beer styles we do not commonly see anymore, but are without a doubt worthy of celebration. That's 10 beers in the Historical Beers style. Try 5 more for level 3.

items.checkin_comment: Lager with a little extra tang/bitterness. Not malty like I expected

That's a beer at 25 different venues categorized as a Hotel! Visit 5 more different ones for Level 6!

items.badge_description: Sometimes, it seems that we are as dedicated to our American lager of choice as our own football team. Do you go ham for…Hamm's? Grab a Coors Light cold one? Find Milwaukee's Best…best? Whatever it is and wherever you are: Here's to your stadium beer!

There's beer in them thar hills! You're setting off on your journey through the world of beer, but be sure to take a little time to get to know each brewery as you go. That's a beer from 260 different breweries. Try 5 more for Level 53!
